item.title: Sugar industry not sustainable, Govt to take time on price assessment - Immanuel
item.description: Rashika Kumar , Priya Nand · 14/07/2026 She was unable to harvest her cane because of the low delivery payment received by farmers.
item.content:encoded:
Rashika Kumar , Priya Nand · 14/07/2026

She was unable to harvest her cane because of the low delivery payment received by farmers.

She says fuel prices have made it difficult for many to pay lorry operators to transport cane from Seaqaqa to the Labasa Mill.

Maisiri also says many cane farmers are still waiting for the promised guaranteed cane price of $110 per tonne , which was promised by the National Federation Party.

However, NFP Leader, Professor Biman Prasad earlier last week rejected claims that NFP promised $110 cane price.

item.title: From military upbringing to rugby captain: Bitila Tawake will share her journey on fijivillage Her-Story tomorrow at 7pm
item.description: Ahead of the McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women’s historic Super Rugby Women’s home semi-final against the Queensland Reds this weekend, a special episode of fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono will feature captain Bitila Tawake, who will share her journey from growing up in a strict military household to becoming one of Fiji’s leading female rugby players. Tawake reflects on balancing leadership wit
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of the McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women’s historic Super Rugby Women’s home semi-final against the Queensland Reds this weekend, a special episode of fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono will feature captain Bitila Tawake, who will share her journey from growing up in a strict military household to becoming one of Fiji’s leading female rugby players.

Tawake reflects on balancing leadership with being a player, overcoming criticism in a male-dominated sport, missing family milestones in pursuit of her rugby dreams and the importance of inspiring the next generation of female athletes.

Don't miss the interview with Tawake on fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono, at 7pm tomorrow.

item.title: Keep harvesting despite low cane price - Viam Pillay
item.description: While opposing the 2026/2027 National Budget in Parliament, Pillay says although the Government's fuel subsidy is welcomed, it does not fully cover farmers' costs, particularly for those in remote areas. He says after paying for harvesting, farm inputs, land preparation and lease costs, farmers are left with very little before they even pay to transport their cane to the mills.
item.content:encoded:
While opposing the 2026/2027 National Budget in Parliament, Pillay says although the Government's fuel subsidy is welcomed, it does not fully cover farmers' costs, particularly for those in remote areas.

He says after paying for harvesting, farm inputs, land preparation and lease costs, farmers are left with very little before they even pay to transport their cane to the mills.

Pillay says once transport costs are added, many farmers are left with little or no profit.

He says this season has been made even more difficult by lower cane production, caused by factors such as bad weather and poor infrastructure.

He is calling on the Government to review the guaranteed cane price and provide greater support for farmers.

Pillay also called for a special audit of the Fiji Sugar Corporation, claiming there is a need for greater accountability and lower operating costs.

item.title: Council garbage truck driver under investigation after fatal incident
item.description: Police are investigating the driver of a Suva City Council garbage truck following an incident in which a worker allegedly jumped onto the moving vehicle, reportedly slipped, and was run over. According to the worker's family, he was carrying out his cleaning duties in Nasese when he was allegedly chased by a pack of dogs, prompting him to jump onto the moving truck.
item.content:encoded:
Police are investigating the driver of a Suva City Council garbage truck following an incident in which a worker allegedly jumped onto the moving vehicle, reportedly slipped, and was run over.

According to the worker's family, he was carrying out his cleaning duties in Nasese when he was allegedly chased by a pack of dogs, prompting him to jump onto the moving truck.

Police have confirmed to fijivillage News that once investigations are over, they will release a statement.

The Suva City Council says they are also conducting their own investigations and they are working with the Accident Compensation Commission of Fiji to compensate the victim's family.

item.title: Fuel supply returns to normal in Taveuni as shipping resumes
item.description: Speaking to fijivillage News, Suncity Supermarket owner Rohil Chaudhari says fuel supplies have now returned to normal, with service stations across the island fully stocked. Chaudhari says another fuel shipment is expected to arrive tomorrow after the first shipment arrived last week.
item.content:encoded:
Speaking to fijivillage News, Suncity Supermarket owner Rohil Chaudhari says fuel supplies have now returned to normal, with service stations across the island fully stocked.

Chaudhari says another fuel shipment is expected to arrive tomorrow after the first shipment arrived last week.

He says other essential goods affected by the shipping disruption are also expected to be restocked over the next few days.

Chaudhari says with regular shipping services now restored, the island is expected to return to normal within the next week .

item.title: Ratu Naiqama calls on Lau to extend their fishing boundaries to earn more
Diaspora Policy to strengthen ties with Fijians overseas - Qereqeretabua
item.description: The Government will implement Fiji's Diaspora Policy in the new financial year to strengthen engagement with Fijians living overse

item.title: It is an irresponsible budget from an oversized cabinet in an overspending Government - Opposition MP Sharma
item.description: The 2026-2027 National Budget is an irresponsible budget from an oversized Cabinet in an overspending Government. This has been stated by Opposition Member of Parliament Rinesh Sharma this morning in parliament during the Budget debate.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026-2027 National Budget is an irresponsible budget from an oversized Cabinet in an overspending Government.

This has been stated by Opposition Member of Parliament Rinesh Sharma this morning in parliament during the Budget debate.

Sharma criticised the Coalition Government’s fiscal strategy, questioning its decision to describe the Budget as a responsible budget while highlighting the projected deficit, rising debt and increased government expenditure.

Sharma says the Budget is weak fiscal management, pointing to the Government’s projected revenue of $3.8 billion and expenditure of $4.8 billion, resulting in a $1 billion deficit.

He says when debt repayments of $436 million are included, the gross deficit rises to $1.4 billion.

He questioned how the Government can claim fiscal responsibility while continuing to borrow, saying borrowing should focus on capital projects that generate returns rather than supporting operational costs.

Insert: Sharma - Wake them up to reality July 14

He further says the Government must focus on creating opportunities for young Fijians through better education pathways, affordable housing and stronger support for businesses.

Sharma proposed a Government-supported affordable housing model aimed at helping young families and first-time home buyers access housing while the State retains ownership of land.

He also called for greater youth involvement in national decision-making, saying young people should have a meaningful role in shaping Fiji’s future.

item.title: $4B worth of illicit drugs circulating in Fiji over 3 years - Naivalurua
item.description: With more than $4 billion worth of illicit drugs circulating in Fiji over the past three years, Minister for Policing and Communic

item.title: Off-field success overshadowed by Flying Fijians' on-field struggles
item.description: While the off field result has been a tremendous success, the on field result of the last two games of the Fiji Water Flying Fiji
